name: Student Life
summary: Student Life oversees all registered student organizations (including fraternities/sororities, honor societies, and Graduate Student Associations). Student Life also coordinates many other aspects of the student experience.

name: Environmental Sciences mini-Graduate Student Association
summary: To provide a unified voice of ES graduate students within the Graduate Student Association by enhancing interaction and communication amoung the interdiciplinary student body that makes up the ES department.

name: Bioengineering Mini-GSA
summary: Bioengineering Mini-Graduate Student Association

name: Engineers Without Borders at UCR
summary: Engineers Without Borders is a non-profit humanitarian organization established to partner with developing communities worldwide in order to improve their quality of life. This partnership involves the implementation of sustainable engineering projects.

name: American Association for Aerosol Research Student Chapter at UCR
summary: AAAR Student Chapter fosters a community for which students can exchange scientific ideas about aerosol research.

name: Society for Advancement of Chicanos and Native Americans in Science
summary: We, the members of SACNAS at UCR, established to foster the success of Hispanic/Chicanx/Latinx and Native American scientists to attain advanced degrees, careers, and positions of leadership.

name: Tau Beta Pi (The Engineering Honor Society)
summary: Founded in 1885, Tau Beta Pi is the only honor society that represents all engineering disciplines. This prestigious honor society accepts members with distinguished scholarship and exemplary character as students in engineering.

name: Graduate Student Association
summary: The Graduate Student Association exists to advance, through our advocacy and programs, the academic, social, and physical environment of current and future graduate students at the University of California, Riverside.

name: Chinese Students and Scholars Association
summary: To Promote understanding and cooperation among Chinese students and scholars as well as with other groups in the university.

name: Pakistani Student Association
summary: We are a student organization open to all undergraduate & graduate students, staff, and faculty. We seek to bring together those members of the community that share our interest in the social, cultural, and political milieu of Pakistan.

name: Chemistry Graduate Student Association at UCR
summary: Organization for chemistry graduate students to socialize, professionalize, and spread an interest in chemistry to the community.

name: Best Buddies at UCR
summary: Best Buddies Colleges fosters one-to-one friendships between college students and adults with IDD and college students without IDD. Best Buddies is providing the opportunity for people with IDD to be involved in campus and community.

name: Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) at UCR
summary: ACM at UCR is a technical organization with a focus on computer science that is open to all years and majors.

name: Student Affiliates in School Psychology
summary: Student Affiliates in School Psychology

name: Neuroscience Graduate Student Association
summary: We vow to be an academic/social organization, with its own independent finances, empowered to represent graduate students, and to affect academic program policy.

name: Running Club at UCR
summary: We’re a casual, social, running club based at UCR, open to anyone regardless of experience level! Providing a friendly, welcoming, and safe environment for everyone. Join us outside the SRC at 4pm Mon-Fri, no commitment required!

name: Art History Undergraduate Association
summary: The purpose of the Art History Undergraduate Association is to enhance involvement in the field of Art History among interested UCR students through meetings, lectures, field trips, films, and other events with an art historical focus.

name: Graduate Students of Political Science
summary: The Graduate Students of Political Science seeks to provide all graduate students in our department with the organizational structure and support to advance their needs and experience.

name: National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC)
summary: In alignment with its mission, the National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC) at UCR is dedicated to advancing the well-being of its affiliated fraternities and sororities through leadership and professional development opportunities for its members.

name: African Americans United in Science (AAUS)
summary: The purpose of AAUS is to increase the educational and professional opportunities available to African American science students at the University of California, Riverside; stimulate the dissemination of valuable information to these students

name: TEDxUCR
summary: In the spirit of ideas worth spreading, TED has created a program called TEDx. It is a program of local, self-organized events that bring people together to share a TED-like experience. Our event is call TEDxUCR, where x=independently organized TED event

name: National Society of Black Engineers
summary: Our mission is to increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community.

name: Black Student Union
summary: To provide a space for all students to gain personal knowledge and information about African/African American/Black populations in an effort to help bring down the cultural barriers and stereotypes that have plagued the Afrikan minority for centuries.

name: Kendo Club @ UCR
summary: We meet and practice Kendo, no prior experience necessary.

name: Genetics, Genomics and Bioinformatics Graduate Student Association
summary: The GGB GSA exists to further the goals and interests of graduate students within the Genetics, Genomics, and Bioinformatics graduate program. We  organize both scholarly and social events and discussions to benefit our overall graduate school experience

name: Associated Students Program Board
summary: The office of ASPB is dedicated to programming quality campus entertainment for the undergraduate population at the University of California, Riverside that appeals to the diverse communities and enhances campus life.

name: The Mini-Graduate Student Association of the Environmental Toxicology Program
summary: The purpose of the UCR ETOX mini-GSA is to provide a unified voice of the ETOX graduate students within the larger Graduate Student Association and foster a community where ETOX students can easily interact with each other both academically and socially.

name: CA Public Interest Research Group (CALPIRG)
summary: CALPIRG is a statewide, student run, and student directed non-profit advocacy group that provides service through educating and mobilizing students on issues like protecting our environment, addressing basic needs, and making college more affordable.

name: Student Association of Graduate Anthropologists
summary: Graduate students are encouraged to share ideas and raise awareness regarding our Anthropology Department in SAGA. The purpose of SAGA is to foster a community of support that will help our graduate students succeed intellectually and professionally.

name: Katipunan Pilipino Student Organization
summary:
Katipunan is an organization at UCR that seeks to advance the awareness and development of the Filipino/American culture through cultural, academic, community, and social means.

Feel free to contact us at ucrkatipunanpresident@gmail.com

name: Undergraduate Business Association
summary: Undergraduate Business Association is dedicated to creating a strong and ever-growing network of professional connections as a vehicle to spread our passion for business and advance the professional goals of our members.

name: Student Alumni Association
summary: SAA is run by students, for students. In today's competitive job market employers are looking for more than just academic achievements, and SAA gives you the chance to build your resume.

name: Music Changing Lives at UCR
summary: Music Changing Lives at UCR was established to expand the humanities and arts through community outreach, volunteering, social awareness campaigns, and fundraisers.

name: Accounting Society at UC Riverside
summary: The Accounting Society aims to promote the educational and professional experience of accounting students at UCR by giving members exposure to internship/full-time opportunities to work with accounting firms and government agencies.

name: American Marketing Association at UCR
summary: A business student run organization focused on developing students into successful leaders in the field of marketing, management, advertising, public relations, sales, and other related fields. We participate in professional events, workshops, and others

name: Microbiology Graduate Student Association
summary: MicroGSA is dedicated to fostering a collaborative and supportive community for current and future graduate students within the Microbiology Graduate Program.

name: Student Athlete Advisory Committee
summary: 1st and 3rd Monday of the month

name: Chemical and Environmental Engineering Graduate Student Association
summary: Social Organization for Chemical and Environmental Engineering Graduate Students

name: National Residence Hall Honorary
summary: To aid the development and maintenance of a strong, diverse and academically successful residential community within the UCR Residence Halls.

name: Graduate Students in English Association
summary: Our primary purpose is to ensure the welfare and representation of graduate students' concerns at all relevant levels of the English Department's business.

name: Mujeres Unidas de UCR
summary: We are concerned with educational, social and economic issues within our community while empowering la mujer of UCR.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
summary: We obtain excellence through integrity, empowerment, achievement, diversity, and continuous improvement. We are the leading social-technical organization whose primary function is to enhance and achieve the potential of Hispanics in STEM.

name: Delta Gamma
summary: Article II: To foster high ideals of friendship among college women, to promote their educational and cultural interests, to create in them a true sense of social responsibility, and to develop in them the best qualities of character

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers (AIChE)
summary: AIChE is a professional affiliation consisting of Chemical and Environmental Engineers, but all majors are welcome to join!

name: Taiwanese Graduate Student Association
summary: Provides networking, educational resources, and information exchange to support member development.

name: Music Graduate Student Association
summary: The purposes of this organization shall be: a) to serve as a medium of communication between members of the MGSA, the GSA, and the Music Department; b) to promote and coordinate the interests of the members of the MGSA; c) to facilitate valuable intellect

name: Highlander Mock Trial
summary: We are a student-run organization that enables members to train and compete in trial tournaments hosted by The American Mock Trial Association. Check out our instagram @ucrmocktrial or email us at mocktrialucr@gmail.com for more information!

name: Society of Women Engineers
summary: SWE at UCR is a professional engineering organization specializing in professional and academic development, increasing the female presence in BCOE, and outreach opportunities by encouraging young students to pursue STEM fields.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Pre-Law Fraternity
summary:
Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International is a professional law fraternity
advancing integrity, compassion and courage through service to the student, the school,
the profession and the community.

name: Earth and Planetary Science Graduate Student Association
summary: The EPS-GSA represents the graduate students of the Earth and Planetary Science Department.

name: Providing Opportunities Dreams and Education in Riverside
summary: Established in 2008, PODER seeks to provide assistance to undocumented students through mentorship, financial assistance, and community building.

name: Chemistry Club
summary: Our goal is to show, the students within the UCR community, the many opportunities in the field of chemical sciences, networking, and outreach for higher-level education in the sciences. If you have any questions, send us an email! Open to ALL majors!

name: Art History Graduate Student Association
summary: Graduate Student Organization open to all graduate students in the History of Art Department at the University of California, Riverside

name: Theta Tau Professional Engineering Fraternity - Sigma Delta Chapter
summary: The purpose of this fraternity shall be to develop and to maintain a high standard of professional interest among its members, and to unite them in a strong bond of fraternal fellowship.

name: California Alliance for Minority Participation
summary: The California Alliance for Maximizing Potential (CAMP) is designed for students in the Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics [STEM] fields pursuing graduate (Master/Doctoral) degrees.

name: Associated Students of UCR
summary: The Associated Students of University of California, Riverside (ASUCR) is the official student government at UCR representing over 18,000 undergraduates.

name: Commuter Programs at UCR
summary: Commuter Programs at UCR aims to help those students that walk / bus / bike / drive / or otherwise travel to get to campus.

name: Highlander Orientation
summary: Highlander Orientation is a program for incoming first-year students. This page is intended for Orientation Leaders, student staff, and the professional staff affiliated who work with Highlander Orientation.

name: Student Disability Resource Center (SDRC)
summary: Student Disability Resource Center (SDRC) is committed to an accessible, equitable, and inclusive experience for students with disabilities through advocacy, empowerment, and collaboration with the UCR Community to reduce barriers for all students.

name: Women's Resource Center
summary: The Women's Resource Center is a multifunctional Student Affairs Department that offers programs and services around issues concerning women and gender.  For more information, please visit http://wrc.ucr.edu.

name: LGBT Resource Center
summary: The LGBT Resource Center provides support, education, and advocacy regarding sexual orientation and gender identity for the UC Riverside campus community.

name: UCR Go
summary: Join UCR's GoPedometer program, where the more you walk, the more you win !

name: Student Conduct and Academic Integrity Programs
summary: Student Conduct & Academic Integrity Programs (SCAIP) supports the diverse educational community at UCR by serving as a resource and educating the campus regarding issues of student conduct.  We promote student development, accountablity and success thro

name: Resources and Research in Genders Initiative
summary: The Resources and Research in Genders Initiative (RReGI) is a culmination of ideas between the Women's Resource Center and the Women's Studies Department.  The primary focus is to provide information and opportunities to students in the topic of Genders.

name: The Dynamic Genome Outreach Group
summary: The purpose of The Dynamic Genome Outreach Group, DGOG, is to inspire and educate K-12 students in molecular biology and related research sciences by allowing hands-on experience on model experiments guided by UCR undergraduates.

name: Chicano Student Programs
summary: ¡Bienvenidos a nuestra familia de UCR! We celebrate the traditions of Chicano/Latino culture while sharing the spirit of La Raza with our campus and community. We are one big familia, and like a family, we fully support your success at UCR!

name: Graduate Student Association Teacher Education Program Student Representative Committee
summary: The TEP SRC provides a voice for, and advocates on behalf of the Graduate School of Education TEP's students. We serve as a medium of communication between the UCR GSE administration, the UCR GSA, and all five cohorts within the GSE TEP.

name: TRIO Scholars
summary: The mission of the UCR TRiO Scholars Program is to promote the academic, social, personal, and professional success of all students by building a strong sense of community and drawing on resources both within & outside the program.

name: Residence Hall Association
summary: Residence Halls Association (RHA) is a student run organization that operates under Resident Life and Housing Services at UCR. RHA works closely with both of these departments to ensure that the greater needs and desires of the residents are heard.

name: Inland Empire Scholar Program
summary: The Inland Empire Scholars are students who will succeed in attaining their education and help make a difference in today's world.

name: Asian Pacific Student Programs
summary: Staff and students work together at APSP to plan and implement activities, events, and programs for you. We also provide a strong community of support, a safe and welcoming environment, and a positive, culturally rich college experience.

name: Highlander Union
summary: The Highlander Union is a gathering place where you & your friends can meet, eat, relax & study. The HUB has its own  programs team who produce events within the HUB area, including Latitude 55, and events such as Chalk the Walk & Heat Music Festival.

name: Native American Student Programs
summary: Welcome to Native American Student Programs, where you can unite as a community with other American Indian students at UC Riverside. This is your home away from home - a place of support, friendship, and belonging. while celebrating your native culture.

name: Project RISHI at UCR
summary: We are a national non-profit organization that works to promote the sustainable growth and development of rural Indian villages.

name: Asian Americans of Riverside Theatre
summary: Celebrating diverse voices through theater and film. Welcome to AART!

name: Hylander Financial Group
summary: Founded in 2013, Hylander Financial Group (HFG) was established to help UCR students learn successful investing techniques, build networks in finance, and provide practical knowledge to the UCR community about the financial industry.

name: Secret Swords and Sorcery Society
summary:
The purpose of this group is to organize tabletop and live role playing games such as D&D.

As of Spring 2024, 
Meetings Times: 6:00pm to 10:00pm Tuesdays and Fridays
Place: Hub 265

Check out our Discord for more info!
 https://discord.gg/53Sp6Qz

name: R'Stage Productions
summary: Student actors, directors, writers, stage managers, and designers of all majors are welcome. R'Stage Productions aims to showcase student work and provide a means for artistic collaboration between students.

name: CHASS F1RST
summary: CHASS F1RST provides students with the tools, opportunities, and experiences to be successful at UCR and beyond.

name: CHASS Transfers First Program
summary: CHASS Transfers F1RST Program aims to provide useful information and resources to help incoming first-year transfer students navigate their life at UCR.

name: A. Gary Anderson Graduate School of Management Student Association
summary: The AGSM student association aims to enhance the graduate experience and lay a foundation for growth by supporting a network of students, faculty, staff, alumni and the UCR community.

name: Change in Scientific Importance for Youth
summary: Delta SIFY aims to provide immersive educational opportunities for students in underserved communities to explore the fields of STEM. We believe that access to a quality, fulfilling education should be a fundamental human right, not a privilege.

name: National Black Law Student Association
summary: Established to unite students with their peers who have a similar interest in the legal field, to provide students a better understanding of careers within the legal field, and to give opportunities for mentorship with current practitioners in law.

name: CSGSA
summary: Graduate Student Association for Dept. of Computer Science graduate students

name: Club Baseball at UCR
summary: Club Baseball at UCR strives to create an inclusive environment for baseball fanatics while also promoting competition on the field. We host games, socials, and trips to MLB games to create a community for all UCR baseball lovers.

name: The Literacy Initiative
summary: The purpose of this organization is to raise child literacy rates in the Riverside community to the child’s respective grade reading level or above through an incentive program.

name: Math Graduate Student Association
summary: Graduate Student Association for the Department of Mathematics

name: Dean of Students
summary: Our staff of educational professionals all have one thing in common: An abiding passion to encourage college students to thrive, while providing the resources for academic and personal success.

name: Astronomy Club @ UCR
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to bring together those throughout UCR and the general public that share a same level of appreciation of the space-related sciences to facilitate a diverse supportive environment.

name: Phi Chi Theta
summary: Phi Chi Theta at UCR is a co-educational business and fraternity dedicated to creating well-balanced leaders through involvement, empowerment, and philanthropy. We are committed to building the next generation of business leaders.

name: AGSM Women in Business
summary:
The purpose of this organization is to empower aspiring business professionals and to both educate and combat gender inequalities in the professional setting. 

Please send us a message, if you are interested in joining or collaborating with us!

name: Healthy Hearts Student Organization at UCR
summary: It shall raise awareness of heart related illnesses and to prevent the spread of heart related illnesses by educating the public on Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR) protocol, lifestyle modifications information, and stress relief technique

name: Campus Advocacy, Resources & Education
summary: CARE (Campus Advocacy, Resources & Education) is an intervention and prevention support program dedicated to ending sexual violence at UC Riverside.

name: AISES Chapter at UC Riverside
summary: To advance the objectives of the American Indian Science and Engineering Society in its own geographical location. To nurture building of community by bridging science and technology with traditional native values.

name: Community Engaged Learning
summary:
"Where academia meets the community"

UCR Community Engaged Learning is an educational initiative integrating community service, service learning through courses, academic internships, and community-based research!

name: Statistics Graduate Student Association
summary: The official GSA of the Statistics Department's graduate program.

name: Cybersecurity at the University of California, Riverside
summary: We hope to encourage beginners and experts alike to learn more about physical and digital security. Cyber@UCR provides multiple avenues for students to be involved in technology. Whether you are a computer science major or an arts major, all is welcome!

name: Unheard Cries Charity
summary: The purpose of this club is to spread awareness of Middle Eastern penury and persecution. This organization will fight poverty through specific programs that provide humanitarian assistance for people in need.

name: Academic Intervention Program
summary: Brief summary here.

name: Tutorial Assistance Program
summary: The Tutorial Assistance Program (TAP) offers free undergraduate tutoring in various subjects and disciplines. Students can make appointments online at arc.ucr.edu or come in for drop-in tutoring in select subjects, including math and chem.

name: EARS Student Group
summary:
MUSIC AND TECHNOLOGY PRODUCTION GROUP. We make sounds sound beautiful.

We want to apply YOUR UC education to the world of audio and acoustics! We're pushing the limits of music technology, production, artistic expression, and much more.

name: Physics Organization for Womxn and the Under-Represented
summary: POWUR is an organization committed to supporting under-represented members of the Physics department at UCR.

name: Southeast Asian Studies Graduate Student Association
summary: SEASGRAD is an association of graduate students affiliated with the Southeast Asian Studies program (SEATRiP) at UC Riverside. Our mission is to build an inclusive, empowered and professional community of scholars in Southeast Asian Studies.

name: Leadership in Action Certificate
summary: The Leadership in Action certificate is a program based on the Social Change Model of Leadership Development—a theoretical model that approaches leadership on 3 separate levels; the individual, the group, and the society.

name: Women in Computing at UCR
summary: We aim to support, celebrate, and empower women, non-binary, and underrepresented groups in computing at UCR.

name: STAGE: Chinese Performing Arts Club
summary: STAGE is an organization that students could learn about Chinese performing arts such as Chinese drama.

name: A Friend in Me
summary: A Friend in Me is an organization dedicated to providing pediatric cancer patients with a sense of normalcy and friendship. This club provides members with volunteering, networking, and patient-interaction opportunities, fostering professional growth.

name: Neuroscience Club
summary: This club is meant for people with a common interest in the brain, its fuctions, and affect on thought and behavior. We hope to provide a place to discuss our common interest and provide a more interactive, fun resource for learning about the brain.

name: Collective on Violence Prevention
summary: The Collective on Violence Prevention (CVP) is a student network of diverse men and leaders on campus dedicated to ending sexual violence at UC Riverside. CVP seeks to create spaces on campus that explore and promote healthy masculinities.

name: Center for Conservation Biology
summary: We are an interdisciplinary group of students stemming from various departments, coming together to promote theta conservation and restoration of species and ecosystems. We also provide opportunities for outreach and professional development.

name: First Generation Student Association
summary: FGSA hopes to empower first generation students by creating a community that promotes academic success and networking. We look to support and create unity with all of our members for success.

name: HOSA - Future Health Professionals
summary: UCR HOSA is a pre-health organization that serves students in serving UCR and the surrounding community by volunteering, networking, and socializing with students alike. We compete in leadership conferences in order to better prepare for future careers.

name: Young Democratic Socialists of America at UC Riverside
summary: YDSA is the youth and student section of the Democratic Socialists of America. We believe that working people should run both the economy and society democratically to meet human needs, not to make profits for a few.

name: United Nations International Children's Emergency Fund @ UCR
summary: UNICEF @ UCR is a club that will advocate for the protection of children's rights and will uphold these values through community service, fundraising to support UNICEF’s aid programs, and spreading awareness on a local and global level.

name: R'Brains and Behavior
summary: We are a Psychology Club! Our purpose is to establish resources, such as networking opportunities, educational advancement, and professional development, in addition creating a community for young professionals within the field of psychology.

name: SciComm @ UCR
summary: We practice creative communication of science to the general public through events and multimedia projects. Additionally, we organize an annual workshop about the practice of science communication.

name: School of Business R'LEAD Program
summary: The R’LEAD Program allows students to participate in various professional activities that demonstrate their career investment to employers. Activities include professional development workshops, field trips to various organizations, and networking.

name: Fight For Autism
summary: An action and awareness organization that hosts interactive events and activities to foster inclusivity for autistic individuals and peer advocates in the Riverside community.

name: Student Chapter of ISPE at UCR
summary: The International Society for Pharmaceutical Engineering (ISPE) aims to introduce its members to aspects of the pharmaceutical and biotechnology industries, promote educational/career/internship opportunities, and support personal and professional growth

name: The Women's Network
summary: The Women's Network exists to create a community of women that celebrates the desire to achieve, cultivating their ambition in order to prepare the next generation of leaders. We encourage women to be supportive of one another and build each other up.

name: Creat'R Lab
summary: The Creat'R Lab is an inclusive living state-of-the-art technology incubator for discovery, innovation, and entrepreneurship located in the Orbach Library

name: UCR Library
summary: This summary appears when browsing.

name: Design at UCR
summary: University of California, Riverside’s first & only design club to support students interested in UI/UX, web design, and design thinking.

name: Audeamus
summary: Audeamus was started in 2007 at UC Riverside as a multidisciplinary honors journal for undergraduate students across the University of California (UC) system.

name: Pacific Math Alliance
summary: Are you an aspiring mathematician, or just really love math? PMA aims to help underrepresented students learn and prepare for math-related post-graduation careers, make new math friends, and learn more about mathematics as a whole!

name: Programming Club at UCR
summary: The Programming Club at UCR is a graduate student organization created to help fellow graduate students learn finance and information system industry-specific programs.

name: Highlander Business Society
summary: Highlander Business Society (HBS) is a professional student organization, established on 4 core values to prepare our members for the professional world: Professionalism, Leadership, Community, and Growth.

name: Mujeres Activas en Letras y Cambio Social
summary: Creating a safe space for womxn to speak out on issues affecting them on campus and within the communities, navigate academia and prepare for graduate school, and represent UCR at the annual national MALCS Summer Institute (held during the Summer).

name: Dependents and Military Family
summary: The purpose of this organization is to create a community where dependents and military loved ones can get together and support one another. Our mission is to create a welcoming organization where we can all learn about the Veteran benefits together.

name: Loving our Emotions as Asians & Pacific Islanders
summary: We encourage conversation within the Asian & Pacific Islander community of UCR. We aim to create a safe space to share, process, and acknowledge our emotions in order to normalize all aspects of working towards a healthy state of mind.

name: School of Business Leadership Council at UC Riverside
summary: To increase collaboration among School of Business student organizations and the school administration to deliver excellence to the UCR School of Business community.

name: Womxn In the Law
summary:
The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to create a supportive community and network for diverse womxn pursuing law school. A safe space for minority womxn pre-law undergraduate students that aim to join the legal profession.
Established 2022

name: University Communications
summary: The University Communications team tells the story of America’s fastest rising university.

name: Highlander Consulting Group
summary: Highlander Consulting Group is committed to helping students develop their professional competencies, empowering them with the right opportunities to work directly with other Businesses, and giving them hands-on work experience in Business Consulting.

name: Vain Fashion Club @ UCR
summary: Vain Fashion Club @ UCR

name: Success in Science and Technology: Engagement with Role Models
summary: The objective of our organization is to facilitate group meetings using the role-model approach to encourage middle school girls to attend college and pursue careers in STEM.

name: Goths and Alts of UCR
summary: Our club exists to create an inclusive haven for the exploration of alternative and goth culture, where members can connect through music, art, and aesthetics.

name: Little Explorers In N'terdisciplinary Sciences To Encourage INspiration
summary: Our mission is to expose elementary school students to the interdisciplinary nature of STEM, encourage them to enter higher education, and inspire them to pursue a STEM-related career in the future.

name: BROTHERs
summary: The objective of our organization is to facilitate group meetings using the role-model approach to encourage middle school boys to attend college and pursue careers in STEM.

name: High Performance Computing Club
summary: Club to learn more about high performance computing and related computing infrastructure. Annual participation in the Student Cluster Competition.

name: Health Clinical & Administrative Program
summary: The Clinical & Administrative Program has been created to provide networking opportunities, clinical experiences, and an administrative background for Pre-Health Undergraduate Students at the University of California, Riverside.

name: CNAS Peer Mentors
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to provide mentorship, resources, professional, personal and academic development to CNAS Students with an emphasis on first year students.

name: Pi Sigma Alpha
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to stimulate scholarship and interest in Political Science by providing tangible recognition to students who have excelled in the field.

name: The EcoCAR EV Challenge Team at UC Riverside
summary: Home of UCR's Team for the EcoCAR EV Challenge, where we provide students with hands-on experience related to the development of electric vehicles, promoting electrification, STEM education and career opportunities in the automotive field.

name: Supporting and Mentoring Aspiring Students in Highschool
summary: The goal of SMASH is to increase college enrollment in STEM fields at Ramona High School by providing tutoring and mentoring services.

name: Highlander Economics Club
summary: The Highlander Economics Club is a club founded to build a community of economics majors and any students interested in economics as a whole by providing access to professors, career panels, speaker events, and research opportunities.

name: Art Factory at UCR
summary: A safe place and creative place for artists in which they can take advantage of a set studio time to create multimedia art. We strive to give students the opportunity to connect with their artistic selves and disconnect from academic pressures.

name: Omicron Delta Epsilon Economics Honor Society at UC Riverside
summary: The Omicron Delta Epsilon(ODE) Economics Honor Society is a welcoming community committed to nurturing academic and professional excellence. We're open to more than just Economics and Business majors. ALL MAJORS are welcome and we can't wait to meet you!

name: Taiwanese American Student Association
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to promote cultural awareness, friendship, inclusivity, family, mentorship, and socializing among students.

name: Anti Warfare Warfare Club
summary: The Anti Warfare Warfare Club seeks to conduct analytical and educational wargaming to study conflict, tactics, international relations, military doctrine, and any other subject that connects to warfare.

name: Highlander Jiu Jitsu at UCR
summary: The purpose of Highlander Jiu Jitsu is to improve the lives of its members through the practice of Jiu Jitsu by fostering fitness, a sense of community, and continuous personal growth.

name: Society of Student Biophysicists
summary: Our mission at SSB is to provide students with the opportunities for academic and professional growth in biophysics and related fields, while also being a hub for all things biophysics here at UCR!

name: Cell, Molecular & Developmental Biology Graduate Student Association
summary: Graduate Student Association for members of the CMDB program to interact and network.

name: Artificial Intelligence Student Collective
summary: The AI Student Collective is a national network of student organizations dedicated to providing accessible AI literacy through pre-professional programs and events.

name: STEMivate
summary: STEMivate is an organization founded in 2023 by students at the University of California, Riverside. This organization was built on the foundation of motivating students across Riverside county to pursue a passion in STEM.

name: She Means Business
summary: The purpose is to principles for the professional advancement of its members, stress the principles of equality and respectability for all members, and provide a forum for the interchange between members of this organization and other UCR students.

name: Academic Resource Center
summary: The ARC offers a wide variety of programs & services to support students in being successful here at UC Riverside. Please come visit us on the 1st floor of Skye Hall and see how we can best support you in your academic and personal pursuits here at UCR!

name: The Young Americans for Liberty at UCR
summary: In YAL at UCR, we champion liberty and freedom for all, committed to combating injustices both on campus and globally. Through weekly meetings, we tackle social, political, and economic issues, and advocate against policies that contradict our beliefs.

name: Black Psych Society
summary: B’Psych will connect students with similar backgrounds and create more accessible opportunities to advance their careers through mentorship, networking, and more. B’Psych will strive to unite and welcome Black scholars to the broad field of psychology.

name: MEDDL Club
summary: The MEDDL Club is dedicated to fostering creativity and innovation among students in the field of Mechanical Engineering by encouraging them to make full use of the Mechanical Engineering Design and Development Laboratory (MEDDL)

name: Spectra at UCR
summary: Spectra is a national professional association whose goal is to empower and support queer and trans individuals in mathematics.

name: Real Estate Club
summary: Embodies reliability in the real estate industry, integrating proven business models and leveraging experienced leaders. We foster growth through continuous education within the community, empowering informed decision-making.

name: Kappa Alpha Pi
summary:
The purpose of the Kappa Alpha Pi Fraternity at the University of California, Riverside shall be
to provide students with the opportunity to learn about the Law School application process, Law
School, and the profession of law.

name: Health Professions Association
summary: HPA is an organization that aims to connect the undergraduate students of UC Riverside who are interested in pursuing a career in healthcare through mentorship, social events, professional development events, and community service events.

name: Ethics Bowl at UCR
summary: At this club we prepare for the annual Ethics Bowl by analyzing cases, developing arguments, and researching. During the off-season we house our own tournaments and continue discussing ethical dilemmas.

name: Enlight Education
summary: Enlight Education has been dedicated to cultivating passion and academic excellence in STEM to empower the next generation of innovators and leaders through high-quality education.

name: Data Science Society
summary: The leading student-run Data Science organization on campus. We welcome all students passionate Data Science, as well as those curious to learn more about the field!

name: AscendUCR
summary: Ascend is the largest global network advancing Pan-Asian professionals in the workplace. It’s our mission to build community and ignite change by developing, elevating, and empowering the API leaders of tomorrow.

name: The Care Equity Lens
summary: This organization's goal to raise awareness to the inequalities in healthcare and research that affect many individuals. We aim to inspire current and future generations of doctors and researchers to take action towards improving the healthcare.

name: Designing Dreams
summary: Learn to sew from UCR’s very own sewing club! Join our step-by-step workshops, our volunteer opportunities, and take part in our fun events to show off your sewing creations!

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers at UCR
summary: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers aims to propel those of Asian descent through opportunities in both their career and resume building aspect, filling a hole needed in the UCR community.

name: Turning Point USA at University of California Riverside
summary: WHAT WE BELIEVE: -The United States of America is the greatest country in the history of the world -The US Constitution is the most exceptional political document ever written -Capitalism is the most moral and proven economic system ever discovered

name: Mycology and Uncommon Botanicals
summary:
The club is about exploring the aspects and characteristics of fungi (mushroom) and rare or unique plants.
As an extension, we will be discussing the multidisciplinary applications of fungi and uncommon plants.

name: Students for Exploration and Development of Space
summary: SEDS (Students for the Exploration and Development of Space) is an international student organization dedicated to advancing space exploration, science, and technology.

name: UCR Brain Game Center
summary: At the UCR Brain Game Center,we strive to improve your brain’s fitness. We research, test, and disseminate evidence-based, scientifically optimized brain fitness games that assist you in real life with memory, vision, hearing, learning, and more!

name: Astronomy Union for Reading, Outreach and Research Activities
summary: Promote research culture through journal clubs, talks, workshops, and outreach. Foster meaningful discussions among graduate students, postdocs, and motivated undergraduates to inspire collaboration and innovation in research.

name: Code Compassion at UCR
summary: To bring awareness and increase understanding about the importance of compassion, empathy, and narrative in healthcare among the Inland Empire/Riverside community– particularly for marginalized and underserved populations

name: Sisters Affirming our Social-cultural Identities
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to provide a safe space for black women. S.A.S.I. aspires to do this through programming, outreach, meetings, events, and any other means to ensure the protection of black women on campus.

name: Women in STEM and the Arts
summary: WISAA aims to create a most relaxing space for individuals that identify as women/femmes who are majoring in STEM by providing an artistic outlet.

name: Black Graduate Student Association at UCR
summary: The mission of the BLack GSA is to foster a vibrant and inclusive space that promotes the academic, professional, social, and cultural well-being of Black graduate students at UCR.

name: MAES Student Chapter at UC Riverside
summary: The purpose of this social and academic organization shall be to promote, cultivate, and advance representation of Latino leadership and education in STEM. Our club will serve as a resource for outreach programs toward K-12.

name: Quranic Studies UCR
summary: A Quranic Studies Club focused on teaching Quranic recitation (Tajweed), Tafsir (interpretation), translation, and memorization in a supportive environment, fostering spiritual growth and deepening understanding of Quranic teachings.

name: Highlander Cloud Computing
summary: The goal of Highlander Cloud Computing is to host workshops based on cloud computing as well as host resources for cloud computing on campus.

name: Alzheimers Foundation of America at UCR
summary: To raise awareness, provide education, and foster a supportive community for those affected by Alzheimer's disease as well as inspire individuals to learn about Alzheimer’s disease through advocacy, volunteer efforts, and scientific discussions.

name: Kindred Volunteer Collective
summary: Make an impact! We raise awareness among various causes & uplift student groups through philanthropy & service. Driven by inclusion, integrity, innovation, & passion, we foster a supportive community for positive change.

name: Virtual Reality X
summary: We are a service-oriented organization that uses cutting-edge VR technology to improve the quality of life of seniors in our community and provide invaluable VR resources to at-risk populations through educational content and early dementia screening.

name: The 3E Study
summary: The 3E Study seeks to generate research to improve health and wellbeing for marginalized emerging adults. We aim to spread awareness about cardiometabolic and mental health, mentoring in college, research opportunities, and social determinants of health.

name: PROsthetics Club
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to promote student exposure to the varying applications of prosthetics, engaging students from all engineering and CNAS backgrounds with an emphasis on medicinal applications I.E. creating mock prosthetics.

name: Pi Kappa Phi
summary: Pi Kappa Phi was founded by exceptional men who chose to lead. For more than a century, we have stayed true to our roots, developing the next generation of servant leaders who put service before self and work to improve the world around them.

name: Sidequests
summary: Leave the city, go on that trip. The night is ours. Experience a sidequest.

name: Healthy Heart Nation UCR
summary: Our mission is to advance cardiovascular health awareness and promote health equity on campus and in the local community by uniting students from diverse fields to engage in clinical outreach, education, and service with underserved populations.

name: Materials Science and Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Association
summary: The MSMEGSA supports graduate students in Materials Science and Mechanical Engineering through professional development, networking, and social events, fostering a strong, collaborative community and advocating for student interests within the department

name: Women's Business Association
summary: A supportive and empowering space for college students, particularly women and students who identify with femininity, to explore personal growth, professional development, and creative expression within the field of Business.

name: Mobile Gaming Academy
summary: Our club is dedicated to playing any gams on the phone and/or on a tablet. We cater to beginners, casual, and pros.

name: Barbecue Club
summary: Our goal is to create a community where students can learn the art and science of barbecue, from smoking meats to mastering techniques.

name: redefineD at UCR
summary: Through service, learning, and real-world experience, we aim to help marginalized populations redefine their lives while inspiring students.

name: Track Club at UCR
summary: Track Club at UCR is a community of runners who share a passion for training, improving, and competition together at a competitive level. Our mission is to provide a supportive environment for those who wish to pursue cross country and track.

name: Business Entertainment Association
summary: Connecting students with opportunities in Business Entertainment and sharing insights into how things operate behind companies focused on Entertainment.

name: BlaqOUT
summary: blaqOUT (UCR) is a social club designed to provide community, opportunities, and foster retention and involvement for Black Queer (LGBTQIA+) students at the University of California, Riverside.

name: Anderson Fintech Club
summary: Empowering UCR students to navigate the future of finance & tech. We connect members with industry leaders to foster responsible innovation, integrity, and accessible banking.

name: Next Generation Graduates
summary: Next Generation Graduates empowers students to achieve post-undergraduate goals through accessible workshops, mentorship, and resources that prepare them for success in graduate and professional programs across healthcare, business, and beyond.

name: Creator Club
summary:
Open Minds, Creative Minds, Free Minds

Come to Creator Club to learn how to go viral on social media, build a life long audience, make money through UGC and more! Come to Creator Club to meet some of your favorite online influencers!

name: School on Wheels at UCR
summary: School on Wheels is a nonprofit organization that provides free one-on-one tutoring, mentorship, and academic support to children experiencing homelessness with the mission of helping these students succeed despite the challenges of housing insecurity.

name: DKMS at UCR
summary: We are an organization dedicated to fighting blood cancer and disorders by creating awareness and recruiting stem cell donors to provide a second chance at life to those in need.

name: Math Club
summary: The goal of the club is to give undergraduates a community with others who enjoy math and to participate in mathematical discussion in an environment that is more relaxed than other mathematical settings.

name: Music Undergraduate Student Association
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be for potential and enrolled Music undergraduate students interested in pursuing a career in Music and beyond to network and support each other's goals and interests. We’ll strive on being the bridge for career

name: Young Peoples Alliance at UC Riverside
summary: As young people, we've seen firsthand how our generation's interests are sidelined by our political system. Our mission is to empower young people to shape their future, engage with the political sphere, and reclaim the American Dream they were promised.

name: Free Enterprise, Entrepreneurship and Networking club
summary: Understand product-market fit and how to validate ideas. Learn the principles of user-centered design and prototyping Combine product, design, and strategy into a cohesive business concept.

name: Neurotechnology Society at UC Riverside
summary: Highlanders exploring and innovating at the intersection of technology, neuroscience, medicine, and bioethics.

name: Wallflower Magazine
summary: Wallflower is an indie life and style magazine redefining quiet confidence. Each issue blends refined aesthetics with expressive experimentation exploring fashion, culture, and personal expression through the eyes of the community learning to stand out.

name: Business Brilliance
summary: Business Brilliance (BB) connects student leaders with peers and professionals to promote growth, leadership development, and international networking.

name: Highlander Cards
summary: Highlander Cards is an organization at the UCR campus focused on building a community amongst students who are interested in learning about card games such as (but not limited to) Uno, Texas Hold'em, and Pot limit Omaha.

name: Wayward Academy: World of Nintendo
summary: A group of people that love Nintendo Games and welcome those who want to play!

name: Association for Women in Mathematics Chapter at UCR
summary: The Association for Women in Mathematics (AWM) at UCR fosters a supportive and inclusive community, promoting equity, professional growth, and engagement for women and underrepresented genders in mathematics at UC Riverside.

name: Echo
summary: Echo is a student-led HiFi and music listening club that explores sound, technology, and culture through shared listening sessions and discussions.

name: Legally Queer
summary: Legally Queer is a space for queer students & allies at UCR who have an interest in law or in studying queer law and history. We host a variety of events including academic, social, and mock trials for students to learn about their legal journey.

name: Women in Film
summary: WIF aims to bring support, education, and a collective voice to women and gender minority filmmakers, through workshops, discussions of women in the industry, movie screenings, and offering production opportunities.

name: NeuroPsychology Club at UCR
summary: Build bridges between the neuroscience and psychology field.

name: Special Olympics - UC Riverside Chapter
summary: This is UCR's campus-based club of the Special Olympics program. Our goal is to connect college students and community members with Special Olympics athletes to create an inclusive community. Our focus is on increasing inclusion and campus impact!

name: Pediatric Hope Project
summary: The Pediatric Hope Project provides a free tutoring program connecting K-12th-grade pediatric patients with volunteer college students. The aim is to supply educational support during treatment.

name: The Reach Initiative
summary: The Reach Initiative is a student led organization serving underserved communities in Riverside, with a focus on the elderly and individuals with disabilities. Through hands on service and advocacy, we build real connections and create lasting impact.

name: Tink'R Lab
summary: The Tink'R Lab offers students opportunities to use maker space equipment to create inspiring projects through innovative workshops! From simple creative endeavors to detailed research, we are open to everyone being able to have a space to create!

name: Break and Build Engineering
summary: Get experience with fun building activities such as breaking down old technologies and rebuilding them, build a bridge activity using different materials and more!

name: Latines in the Mathematical Sciences
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to encourage students to view the Math Department and math world as a whole as a space that is friendly, inclusive, and welcoming to all.

name: Thrive
summary: Our mission is to promote a community for UCR School of Business students that raises conversations and builds support around mental health struggles.

name: Women’s Water Polo
summary: The women water polo club will be  collegiate club that trains at UCR and competes within the state and out of state.

name: Career Accelerator at UCR
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to provide students with a welcoming place on campus to explore careers together through peer support, mentorship, and career events. The goal is to make career preparation feel less overwhelming.

name: Project Pulmonary Riverside
summary: PPM Riverside is a community-based organization established for the purpose of promoting public awareness regarding the harmful effects of carcinogen exposure and other wildfire-related hazards on firefighters and other first-responders.

name: Global Opportunities Abroad Leaders Initiative
summary: The GOALies program is focused on organizing student volunteers who wish to give back to their community, fostering cultural connections formed through experiences around the world, and giving students a resource to learn more about studying abroad.
